engine: vLLM XPU TP4 with llm-scaler unsigned-u4 MoE decode path
experiment: Synchronized rank-0 compiled decode timing summary
purpose: Separate the remaining MiniMax decode ceiling after the custom u4 MoE bridge from vLLM scheduler and MoE-specific overhead.

runs.name: bf16_p512n128_sync_timing_rank0_print512
runs.finding: Periodic MoE prints showed steady rank-0 router_select about 0.06 ms/layer, quant_apply about 0.18 ms/layer, and llm_scaler_u4_bridge about 0.10 ms/layer under synchronized timing.

timing.note: This run accidentally printed every per-layer event before the PRINT_EVERY=0 helper bug was fixed, but the final summary is valid.

interpretation.moe_estimate: Steady router_select plus quant_apply is roughly 0.24 ms per layer, or about 15 ms/token across 62 layers under synchronized timing. quant_apply includes the llm-scaler bridge.
interpretation.remaining_ceiling: Steady runner.forward is roughly 45 ms/token in this diagnostic, so the post-u4 remaining ceiling is mostly outside the custom MoE bridge: attention/KV, Q/K RMS plus TP collectives, projections, and compiled graph boundaries.
interpretation.prefill_cost: The p512/n64 summary still shows 186 fused_experts_fallback calls totaling 815 ms from prefill chunks. This is less important for long decode, but it matters for short-output measurements.

localmaxxing.reason: Diagnostic timing run with synchronization overhead; not a valid throughput benchmark.

next_steps: Keep the timing helper summary patch for future diagnostics.
Do not spend more time optimizing the raw u4 matvec alone until attention/QK/TP boundaries are better understood.
Future high-value work is an XPU analogue of vLLM's CUDA minimax_allreduce_rms_qk, or a communicator-level fusion that removes Q/K variance allreduce plus RMS scheduling overhead.
More RAM or a second NVMe would not improve decode speed directly, but would shorten each MiniMax experiment by avoiding repeated 112 GB NTFS cold loads.
